claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know their age, and if you have multiple children, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, thanks for helping me with this. I've got one child - my son Alex. He's 9 years old, so definitely under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thanks for letting me know about Alex! Since he's 9 years old (under 16), he automatically qualifies in terms of age. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

Given that Alex is 9 years old, I'd assume he's not in paid employment - but please confirm this for me.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all - he's just at primary school. So yes, definitely working less than 24 hours a week!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense - Alex is at primary school and not working. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, given Alex's age (9 years old), I'd expect this wouldn't apply - but please confirm that he's not claiming any such benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he doesn't get any benefits in his own name. He's only 9, so no, nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that makes sense for a 9-year-old. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

Given he's 9 and at primary school, I'd assume not - but just to confirm: Alex is **not** doing any kind of apprenticeship, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - he's way too young for that! He's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! At 9 years old, Alex is definitely not in an apprenticeship. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **one** of these to qualify:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You usually reside in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Pre-settled status and job seeking** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. So yes, option 1 applies to me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, you normally live in the UK - that satisfies the residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about your responsibility for Alex:**

Are you responsible for Alex? You only need to meet **one** of these criteria:

1. **Living with Alex** - Alex lives with you
2. **Contributing to his upkeep** - You contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards his care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, Alex lives with you - that confirms you're responsible for him. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

- **No one else is claiming** for Alex
- **Someone else was claiming**, but they've agreed to transfer the claim to you
- **There's a dispute** - someone else is also trying to claim for Alex and you haven't agreed who should receive it

Which of these applies?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, no other claimants - that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex's living situation:**

Where is Alex currently living? Is he:

- **In hospital** - Currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - Being looked after by a local authority
- **Fostered** - Living with foster parents
- **Living normally** - At home with you (normal living arrangement)

Which of these describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's living normally - at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Lovely, Alex is living at home with you in a normal arrangement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **NOT** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, are you fully supporting Alex yourself without the local council contributing to his living costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - the council isn't paying anything towards him. I support him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That completes the assessment. Let me record the outcome for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Summary for Alex:
✅ Under 16 years old (9 years old)
✅ Not working 24+ hours per week
✅ Not receiving benefits in his own right
✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)
✅ You're responsible for Alex (he lives with you)
✅ No one else is claiming for him
✅ Living at home in a normal arrangement
✅ Local council is not paying towards his care

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Important Note:
⚠️ If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be subject to the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of it may need to be repaid through a tax charge.

**Start your claim here:** https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) He is under 16 years old (9 years old), which means he automatically qualifies on age grounds; (2) He is not working 24+ hours per week; (3) He is not receiving benefits in his own right such as ESA or Universal Credit; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) His parent lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) His parent is responsible for him as Alex lives with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living at home in a normal arrangement; (9) The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They are a UK resident who is fully responsible for a child under 16 living with them at home, with no complicating factors such as other claimants, council support, or special living arrangements.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) He is under 16 years old (9 years old), which means he automatically qualifies on age grounds; (2) He is not working 24+ hours per week; (3) He is not receiving benefits in his own right such as ESA or Universal Credit; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) His parent lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) His parent is responsible for him as Alex lives with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living at home in a normal arrangement; (9) The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They are a UK resident who is fully responsible for a child under 16 living with them at home, with no complicating factors such as other claimants, council support, or special living arrangements.
